OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager in San Francisco to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, as Sensor Tower estimates the share of ChatGPT users aged 35 and older globally rose to 31% in Q2 from 26% a year earlier.

Aldi is pursuing a $9bn US expansion plan to add 800 new stores over five years, targeting dense urban hubs like Manhattan, where the German discounter currently holds just 2.9% of the US grocery market compared to Walmart's roughly 20%.

A woman's body was recovered from the Falls of Falloch near Crianlarich after emergency services - including water rescue units from Oban and Stirling - were called to the 30ft waterfall at about 22:45 on Friday; police said her death was not suspicious.

Software engineers are struggling to adapt as AI reshapes their profession: since ChatGPT's release in 2022, more than 600,000 US tech workers have lost their jobs and US tech job postings on Indeed dropped 36% from 2020 to 2025, according to a Guardian report.

GB grid operator Neso has commissioned an independent legal investigation after a whistleblower alleged that senior bosses ordered control room staff to leave no audit trail of key decisions made during the June heatwave, with grid frequency falling below Neso's operational limits of 49.8Hz-50.2Hz for most of the period between 17:30 and 18:30 on 23 June.

Singapore-based video-generation startup PixVerse closed a Series C extension totalling $439 million, pushing its valuation past $2 billion, with investors including Alibaba, Mirae Asset and BlueFocus; the company says its consumer product has over 150 million registered users and over 15 million monthly active users.

China detained US seismologist Chen Youlin, 54, in November 2024 during a trip to Beijing to visit family; he is currently the only US citizen designated as "wrongfully detained" and his family says he has not been allowed to speak with his wife for over 600 days.
